summary: Keep the live workbench runtime usable through React StrictMode effect rehearsal, stage the verified WASM engine on both browser-acceptance platforms, and stabilize the no-gap banner evidence on its terminal UI state.
specSections: V-4 browser acceptance on Linux and Windows

externalInputs.purpose: Observe exact-head Ubuntu and Windows browser failures and distinguish the StrictMode lifecycle defect, Windows-only missing WASM staging, and cross-state banner measurement race.

decisions.decision: Defer runtime disposal by one microtask and cancel only the pending disposal for the same runtime when React immediately reattaches it. Keep disposal tokens keyed per runtime so a replaced runtime and a truly unmounted runtime are still disposed and cancel active work.
decisions.rationale: React StrictMode rehearses effect cleanup and setup while retaining the memoized runtime. Immediate cleanup permanently marked the live runtime disposed, suppressing later welcome-sample auto-render. Per-runtime deferred disposal distinguishes rehearsal from real lifecycle termination without removing StrictMode or leaking replaced runtimes.

decisions.decision: Download, verify, and stage the retained checksummed OpenSCAD WASM artifact unconditionally in the Linux and Windows browser-acceptance matrix. Continue limiting production-static evidence to Linux while both ordinary browser lanes exercise the real engine.
decisions.rationale: The same staging script and artifact path are already exercised by the Windows parity job. Skipping them in Windows browser acceptance changed four engine journeys into guaranteed unavailable-engine failures and did not satisfy V-4 coverage of the real browser product.

testEvidence.criterion: A welcome sample selected after the engine is ready renders under the production StrictMode mount, while real runtime replacement and unmount still cancel active work.
testEvidence.test: Run focused App integration coverage with engine readiness preceding the welcome click and with unresolved jobs across replacement and unmount.

red.command: npm.cmd test -- tests/app/App.test.tsx -t 'opens a built-in sample from a fresh profile'
red.outcome: RED: under StrictMode, the ready engine received zero render calls after the welcome sample opened, reproducing the hosted Idle state.

green.command: npm.cmd test -- tests/app/App.test.tsx -t 'opens a built-in sample|disposes replaced'
green.outcome: GREEN: 2/2 focused tests passed; the sample rendered, the replaced runtime cancelled old-runtime-render, the replacement remained usable, and real unmount cancelled replacement-render.

testEvidence.criterion: Both Linux and Windows ordinary browser-acceptance lanes stage the exact verified retained WASM artifact.
testEvidence.test: Fail closed on missing named staging steps and reject any platform condition on either e2e staging step.

red.command: GitHub Actions run 29554063544 Browser acceptance (windows-latest); npm.cmd test -- tests/scripts/ci-workflow.test.ts -t 'stages the source-built WASM'
red.outcome: RED: hosted Windows left the engine unavailable in four journeys because both staging steps were Linux-only; the strengthened focused contract failed on that condition.

green.command: npm.cmd test -- tests/scripts/ci-workflow.test.ts -t 'stages the source-built WASM'
green.outcome: FOCUSED GREEN / HOSTED REPLACEMENT PENDING: the fail-closed workflow contract passed 1/1 after both staging steps became unconditional.

testEvidence.criterion: No blank strip exists between the terminal visible engine banner and the workspace on the Windows-supported layout.
testEvidence.test: Wait for the terminal WASM failure alert and Retry control, then poll concurrently sampled banner-bottom and workspace-top geometry.

red.outcome: RED TEST EVIDENCE: sequential loading-to-failure measurements differed by 9 px before the direct workspace-gap invariant was reached.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/c9-settings-layout.e2e.ts -g 'does not reserve a blank project strip'
green.outcome: GREEN: the terminal-state user-facing gap assertion passed 1/1 in Chromium with no CSS or product-layout bypass. Hosted Windows replacement evidence remains pending.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
